abnormalBehaviors.name: Tricophagia
abnormalBehaviors.description: Definition: Eating their own hair or the hair of other horses
abnormalBehaviors.causes:
Common causes:
Boredom (especially in stabled horses)
Nutritional deficiencies (like lack of fiber, minerals)
Stress or lack of environmental enrichment

abnormalBehaviors.name: Biting
abnormalBehaviors.description: it is a normal defense mechanism in stallion
abnormalBehaviors.causes:
causes of biting 
1- Normal defense but when become fixed and repeated it considered a vice.
2- Teasing of animal by stable boys.

abnormalBehaviors.name: Crib biting
abnormalBehaviors.description: A crib-biter swallows air by grasping the edge of the manger or other fixed object with the incisors, raising the floor of the mouth, elevating the soft palate and close the Larynx so the air pass to the stomach.
abnormalBehaviors.causes:
Causes
Isolation for long time without exercise.
Young horse kept accompanied with crib-biter horse.

abnormalBehaviors.name: Litter eating
abnormalBehaviors.description: Eating of bedding material particularly when the litter is in the form of wood particles or straw even it has become soiled.
abnormalBehaviors.causes:
1-Imbalanced ration.
2-feeding at the wrong time of the day.
3-Heavy worm infestation.

abnormalBehaviors.name: Kicking
abnormalBehaviors.description:
Definition: It is the natural method of self-defense among
horses and becomes vices when it is become
habitually and aggressively.
abnormalBehaviors.causes:
Causes :
1-Lack of worband and exereise
2- Teasing of horse.
3-Presence of irritable rodents in dark stable
4-Mare in estrus may show excitement.
5- Disease condition called itchy heel.
6-Nymnphomania due to ovarian dysfunction in
which female showing recurrent estrus.

abnormalBehaviors.name: Pacing
abnormalBehaviors.description:
Horse paces of circles around in loose box .
Animal use the large area afforded by loose box for turning and circle .

abnormalBehaviors.name: Coprophagia
abnormalBehaviors.causes:
*Causes of Coprophagia:*
Considered normal for young foals but abnormal in adult.
Chronic restriction of food.
Isolation for long time without exercise.

abnormalBehaviors.name: Pacing
abnormalBehaviors.description: behavioural disorder in which animal striking the ground with teo of the hoof
abnormalBehaviors.causes:
Causes of Pawing:*
Frustration of animal due to deprivation of food, drink, and sex.
